task_completion.description: The Blender outliner shows an active IFC4 project with a valid spatial hierarchy: IfcProject > IfcSite > IfcBuilding > IfcBuildingStorey.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Blender outliner panel displays a tree structure with IfcProject at the root, containing IfcSite, IfcBuilding, and at least one IfcBuildingStorey node.

task_completion.description: Four external walls forming a closed rectangular building perimeter are visible in the 3D viewport.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 3D viewport shows four walls arranged in a rectangle. The outliner lists at least 4 IfcWall elements. The walls form a closed perimeter without gaps at corners.

task_completion.description: Two internal partition walls divide the building interior into three distinct rooms.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 3D viewport shows internal walls creating three separate enclosed areas within the building perimeter. The outliner shows at least 6 total IfcWall elements.

task_completion.description: A ground floor slab is modeled at the base of the building.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An IfcSlab element appears in the outliner. A horizontal slab surface is visible at ground level in the 3D viewport.

task_completion.description: A flat roof slab is modeled at the top of the building.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A second IfcSlab element appears in the outliner at the roof level. A horizontal surface is visible at the top of the walls in the 3D viewport.

task_completion.description: An entrance door is placed in one of the external walls (south facade).
task_completion.visual_evidence: An IfcDoor element appears in the outliner. A door opening is visible in one of the external walls in the 3D viewport.

task_completion.description: An internal door connects two rooms (retail area to storage).
task_completion.visual_evidence: A second IfcDoor element appears in the outliner. A door opening is visible in one of the internal partition walls.

task_completion.description: Three IfcSpace elements are created with descriptive names for the retail area, storage room, and staff WC.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The outliner shows three IfcSpace elements with names containing words like 'Retail', 'Storage', 'WC', or similar descriptive room labels.

task_completion.description: The project has been saved as retail_unit.ifc in /home/ga/BIMProjects/.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Blender title bar shows a file path containing 'retail_unit.ifc', or a save dialog confirms successful save to the BIMProjects directory, or a file manager view shows the file exists.

integrity.description: The agent used the BlenderBIM GUI for modeling, not Python scripts or terminal commands to generate the IFC file.
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows with ifcopenshell or bpy scripting commands are visible. All modeling was performed through Blender's 3D viewport, toolbars, and properties panels.

integrity.description: The agent did not copy or rename an existing IFC file (such as fzk_haus.ifc) as the output.
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal commands like 'cp' or 'mv' targeting retail_unit.ifc are visible. The model geometry in the viewport is clearly a simple rectangular retail unit, not the FZK-Haus building.

integrity.description: The 3D viewport shows actual building geometry created by the agent, not an empty or default scene.
integrity.visual_evidence: The viewport displays visible wall, slab, and door geometry forming a recognizable rectangular building footprint with interior divisions, clearly different from the default Blender cube scene.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The agent was asked to model a single-storey commercial retail unit (12m x 8m footprint, 3.2m height) starting from a blank IFC4 project with pre-created spatial hierarchy (default Bonsai names: 'My Project' > 'My Site' > 'My Building' > 'My Storey'). Required elements: 4 external walls (200mm thick, forming closed rectangular perimeter), 2 internal partition walls (100mm thick, dividing space into 3 rooms), 1 ground floor slab, 1 flat roof slab, 1 external entrance door (1.0m x 2.1m on south wall), 1 internal door (0.9m x 2.1m between retail and storage), and 3 IfcSpace elements with LongName set to 'Retail Area', 'Storage Room', and 'Staff WC'. All must use correct IFC entity classes (IfcWall, IfcSlab, IfcDoor, IfcSpace) and be contained within the building storey. The file must be saved as /home/ga/BIMProjects/retail_unit.ifc. Software: Blender 4.2.4 LTS with Bonsai 0.8.5-alpha. Available BIM tools include draw_polyline_wall, draw_polyline_slab, add_door, generate_space/generate_spaces_from_walls, save_project. The agent should use the BIM toolbar on the left side and Properties panel on the right. F3 operator search is available as fallback for finding tools. This is a very_hard long-horizon task with 300 max steps.
